food_pairing.name: Chablis grand cru with oysters
food_pairing.pairing: Chablis Grand Cru Les Clos with a plateau of raw oysters
food_pairing.description: Chablis grand cru's oyster-shell minerality and racy acidity are the textbook foil for raw oysters, the saline of each amplifying the other.

food_pairing.name: Meursault with quenelle de brochet
food_pairing.pairing: Barrel-aged Meursault with quenelle de brochet sauce Nantua
food_pairing.description: Meursault's hazelnut, butter and saline weight carry the Lyonnais pike dumpling and its creamy crayfish sauce without being overwhelmed.

food_pairing.name: Gevrey-Chambertin with coq au vin
food_pairing.pairing: Village or premier cru Gevrey-Chambertin with coq au vin
food_pairing.description: A Burgundy braise of rooster in red wine mirrors Gevrey's dark fruit and forest-floor depth, the dish and the wine built from the same region.

food_pairing.name: Pommard with boeuf bourguignon
food_pairing.description: Pommard's iron, firm tannin and savoury depth stand up to slow-braised beef in red-wine reduction, the regional pairing of the Cote de Beaune.

food_pairing.name: Bourgogne Aligote with cervelle de canut
food_pairing.pairing: Bourgogne Aligote with cervelle de canut
food_pairing.description: The herbed fromage blanc of Lyon sharpens Aligote's citrus and saline cut, a fresh, high-acid match that keeps the finish clean.

food_pairing.name: Cote de Nuits Pinot with Epoisses
food_pairing.pairing: Cote de Nuits grand cru Pinot Noir with Epoisses de Bourgogne
food_pairing.description: The pungent washed-rind cow's-milk cheese of Burgundy needs a red with grip and acidity; a structured Cote de Nuits Pinot balances its cream.

food_pairing.name: Village red Burgundy with pate en croute
food_pairing.pairing: Village red Burgundy with pate en croute lyonnais
food_pairing.description: The pastry-wrapped terrine's fat and pistachio echo a supple village Pinot's red fruit and gentle tannin, a classic Lyon wine-bar plate.

food_pairing.name: Corton-Charlemagne with roast turbot
food_pairing.pairing: Corton-Charlemagne grand cru with roast turbot in beurre blanc
food_pairing.description: A powerful grand cru white carries firm white fish and its buttery sauce, citrus and hazelnut framing the richness with a saline lift.

food_pairing.name: Village Chablis with salade lyonnaise
food_pairing.pairing: Unoaked village Chablis with salade lyonnaise
food_pairing.description: Bacon, frisee and a soft poached egg find a foil in unoaked Chablis, the wine's racy acidity and saline edge resetting the palate between bites.

food_pairing.name: Grand cru red Burgundy with roast venison
food_pairing.pairing: Grand cru Cote de Nuits Pinot Noir with roast venison and cassis jus
food_pairing.description: Game meat and a blackcurrant reduction echo a grand-cru Pinot's wild fruit and firm tannin, the centrepiece pairing for Burgundy's greatest reds.
